name: Issue
description: Represents a single issue, ticket, work item, or task in an issue tracking, bug tracking, or project-management system.

id.description: Stable, provider-assigned identifier for the issue.

key.description: Human-readable issue key, often combining a project prefix and a numeric counter.

title.description: Short, one-line summary of the issue.
title.example: OAuth refresh fails when token has trailing whitespace

description.description: Long-form description of the issue, often in Markdown or a provider-specific rich-text format.
description.example: When the stored refresh token contains trailing whitespace, the /oauth/refresh endpoint returns 401 instead of trimming the value.

status.description: Current workflow state of the issue.

priority.description: Importance of the issue relative to other work.

project.description: Identifier of the project, board, or workspace the issue belongs to.

assignee.description: Identifier or email of the user currently responsible for the issue.

reporter.description: Identifier or email of the user who created the issue.

labels.description: Tags or labels classifying the issue.

url.description: Web URL for viewing the issue in its tracker.

updated_at.description: Timestamp the issue was last modified.

due_date.description: Target date for the issue to be resolved.
